model_narrative: We operate the Indus surface-water network over a calendar year, month by month. The network is a system of nodes, river reaches, link canals, reservoirs, and irrigation-canal offtakes. For every month we decide the flow carried on each reach, the water diverted into each irrigation canal, and the end-of-month storage held in each reservoir, together with a single figure for the total stored volume across the system. The objective is to maximize the total live reservoir storage accumulated over all reservoirs and all months.

sets.name: n
sets.doc: all nodes of the river network: barrages, reservoirs, headworks, river-link junctions, and the artificial sea outlet a-sea; reservoir nodes carry live storage, the others are pass-through junctions

sets.name: nb
sets.doc: the nodes that must satisfy a water balance; this is every node except the sea outlet a-sea, which only receives terminal river outflow and is not balanced

sets.name: c
sets.doc: the irrigation canals that draw water off the network at their head nodes

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the system inflow points, the rim stations where the rivers enter the modeled network

sets.name: m
sets.doc: the twelve calendar months in chronological order; the month before january is december, so the reservoir storage cycle wraps around the year

sets.name: s
sets.doc: the two cropping seasons

sets.name: nc
sets.doc: the node-to-canal incidence pairs; a pair means that canal draws its water at that node, so that node loses the canal's diversion

sets.name: nn
sets.doc: the directed reach connectivity pairs; a pair from-node to-node means a river reach or link canal carries flow from the first node to the second

sets.name: ni
sets.doc: the node-to-inflow incidence pairs; a pair means that rim-station inflow enters the network at that node

params.name: ccap
params.doc: the capacity of each canal at its head, in million acre-feet per month

params.name: ncap
params.doc: the transfer capacity of the reach from the first node to the second, in million acre-feet per month; only defined on connected reaches

params.name: rcap
params.doc: the live storage capacity of each node, in million acre-feet; strictly positive only at the reservoir nodes and zero at every pass-through junction, so a positive value is what marks a node as a reservoir

params.name: ircont
params.doc: the initial reservoir contents used to anchor the storage cycle, in million acre-feet, defined at the reservoir nodes

params.name: inflow
params.doc: the rim-station inflow entering the network in each month, in million acre-feet

params.name: runoff
params.doc: the rainfall runoff added at a node in a month, in thousand acre-feet, so it must be divided by one thousand to convert to million acre-feet; defined only at the nodes and months that actually receive runoff, and absent elsewhere

params.name: revapl
params.doc: the evaporation loss from a reservoir in a month, in thousand acre-feet, so it must be divided by one thousand to convert to million acre-feet

params.name: rulelo
params.doc: the lower rule-curve level for a reservoir in a month, expressed as a percent of that reservoir's live capacity

params.name: ruleup
params.doc: the upper rule-curve level for a reservoir in a month, expressed as a percent of that reservoir's live capacity

params.name: trib
params.doc: the tributary inflow from the first node to the second in a month, in million acre-feet; sparse, so a node receives the sum over all source nodes and most entries are absent and count as zero

params.name: coeffl
params.doc: the conveyance efficiency of a link reach from the first node to the second in a month, a multiplier on the flow received at the downstream node; one for ordinary reaches

vars.name: rcont
vars.doc: the end-of-month live storage held at a node, in million acre-feet; meaningful only at reservoir nodes

vars.name: canaldiv
vars.doc: the water diverted into a canal in a month, in million acre-feet

vars.name: f
vars.doc: the flow on the reach from the first node to the second in a month, in million acre-feet

vars.name: vol
vars.doc: the total live reservoir storage summed over all reservoirs and all months, in million acre-feet

constraints.name: canaldiv[c,
constraints.intent: Each irrigation canal can only draw so much water at its head in any month. For every canal and every month, the water diverted into that canal must not exceed the head capacity of that canal.

constraints.name: nn
constraints.intent: Every river reach has a limited carrying capacity. For each reach that actually exists in the network and has a positive transfer capacity, and for every month, the flow carried on that reach from its upstream node to its downstream node must not exceed the capacity of that reach.

constraints.name: rcap[n])
constraints.intent: Each reservoir must be kept at or above its lower rule-curve level. For every reservoir and every month, the end-of-month storage held in that reservoir must be at least the lower rule-curve level for that reservoir in that month, where the level is read as a percent of the reservoir's live capacity. Nodes that are not reservoirs have no such requirement.

constraints.name: rcap[n])
constraints.intent: Each reservoir must be kept at or below its upper rule-curve level. For every reservoir and every month, the end-of-month storage held in that reservoir must not exceed the upper rule-curve level for that reservoir in that month, where the level is read as a percent of the reservoir's live capacity. Nodes that are not reservoirs have no such requirement.

constraints.name: rcap[n])
constraints.intent: The storage cycle is anchored at the start of the water year, which is the month of September. For every reservoir, the end-of-month storage held in September must equal that reservoir's given initial contents. Nodes that are not reservoirs are not anchored.

constraints.name: inflow[i,
constraints.intent: Water is conserved at every balanced node in every month, so everything entering a node equals everything leaving it. Entering a node are the rim-station inflows that arrive there, the local rainfall runoff converted to the same volume units, the tributary inflows arriving from other nodes, and the flows arriving from upstream reaches after applying each reach's conveyance efficiency. Leaving a node are the flows sent out on its downstream reaches and the water diverted into the canals that take off at that node. At a reservoir node the balance also absorbs the change in storage over the month, namely last month's storage minus this month's storage, and subtracts that reservoir's evaporation loss converted to the same volume units. The month before January is December, so the storage carries over around the turn of the year. The sea outlet is not balanced, and a node that has no flow, storage, or diversion at all carries no balance.

constraints.name: vol
constraints.intent: The total stored volume figure adds up all the live storage in the system. Set the total volume equal to the sum of the end-of-month storage over every reservoir and every month.

query: IRSA is floating an anti-concentration rule for the next water year — no single canal should ever pull more than 5% of total monthly canal diversion. What does the optimizer return for the operating plan and system volume under that rule?
expected_pyomo: model.canal_share_5pct = Constraint(model.c, model.m, rule=lambda mm, c, mo: mm.canaldiv[c, mo] <= 0.05 * sum(mm.canaldiv[cc, mo] for cc in mm.c))

query: We're evaluating a minimum-delivery commitment to the provinces: every month the canal network as a whole should divert at least 6 MAF, so downstream allocations are guaranteed. With that floor in place, what does the optimizer return for the plan and total system volume?
expected_pyomo: model.system_canal_floor = Constraint(model.m, rule=lambda mm, mo: sum(mm.canaldiv[c, mo] for c in mm.c) >= 6.0)

query: There's a proposal to hold a combined carryover target on the two big dams — Tarbela plus Mangla together at no less than 13 MAF at the end of September, heading into the rabi season. If we impose that joint September floor, where does the operating plan and total system volume land?

query: A handful of canals carry the diversion mix while plenty of smaller canals sit underused. Couldn't we require that no single canal ever pull more than 8% of total monthly canal diversion? What's keeping the model from spreading the loading out?
expected_pyomo: model.canal_share_8pct = Constraint(model.c, model.m, rule=lambda mm, c, mo: mm.canaldiv[c, mo] <= 0.08 * sum(mm.canaldiv[cc, mo] for cc in mm.c))

params.name: rulelo
params.doc: the lower rule-curve level for a reservoir in a month, expressed as a percent of that reservoir's live capacity

params.name: ruleup
params.doc: the upper rule-curve level for a reservoir in a month, expressed as a percent of that reservoir's live capacity

params.name: trib
params.doc: the tributary inflow from the first node to the second in a month, in million acre-feet; sparse, so a node receives the sum over all source nodes and most entries are absent and count as zero

params.name: coeffl
params.doc: the conveyance efficiency of a link reach from the first node to the second in a month, a multiplier on the flow received at the downstream node; one for ordinary reaches

vars.name: rcont
vars.doc: the end-of-month live storage held at a node, in million acre-feet; meaningful only at reservoir nodes

vars.name: canaldiv
vars.doc: the water diverted into a canal in a month, in million acre-feet

vars.name: f
vars.doc: the flow on the reach from the first node to the second in a month, in million acre-feet

vars.name: vol
vars.doc: the total live reservoir storage summed over all reservoirs and all months, in million acre-feet

constraints.name: canaldiv[c,
constraints.intent: Each irrigation canal can only draw so much water at its head in any month. For every canal and every month, the water diverted into that canal must not exceed the head capacity of that canal.

constraints.name: nn
constraints.intent: Every river reach has a limited carrying capacity. For each reach that actually exists in the network and has a positive transfer capacity, and for every month, the flow carried on that reach from its upstream node to its downstream node must not exceed the capacity of that reach.

constraints.name: rcap[n])
constraints.intent: Each reservoir must be kept at or above its lower rule-curve level. For every reservoir and every month, the end-of-month storage held in that reservoir must be at least the lower rule-curve level for that reservoir in that month, where the level is read as a percent of the reservoir's live capacity. Nodes that are not reservoirs have no such requirement.

constraints.name: rcap[n])
constraints.intent: Each reservoir must be kept at or below its upper rule-curve level. For every reservoir and every month, the end-of-month storage held in that reservoir must not exceed the upper rule-curve level for that reservoir in that month, where the level is read as a percent of the reservoir's live capacity. Nodes that are not reservoirs have no such requirement.

constraints.name: rcap[n])
constraints.intent: The storage cycle is anchored at the start of the water year, which is the month of September. For every reservoir, the end-of-month storage held in September must equal that reservoir's given initial contents. Nodes that are not reservoirs are not anchored.

constraints.name: inflow[i,
constraints.intent: Water is conserved at every balanced node in every month, so everything entering a node equals everything leaving it. Entering a node are the rim-station inflows that arrive there, the local rainfall runoff converted to the same volume units, the tributary inflows arriving from other nodes, and the flows arriving from upstream reaches after applying each reach's conveyance efficiency. Leaving a node are the flows sent out on its downstream reaches and the water diverted into the canals that take off at that node. At a reservoir node the balance also absorbs the change in storage over the month, namely last month's storage minus this month's storage, and subtracts that reservoir's evaporation loss converted to the same volume units. The month before January is December, so the storage carries over around the turn of the year. The sea outlet is not balanced, and a node that has no flow, storage, or diversion at all carries no balance.

constraints.name: vol
constraints.intent: The total stored volume figure adds up all the live storage in the system. Set the total volume equal to the sum of the end-of-month storage over every reservoir and every month.

query: Tarbela gets pulled down to a sliver in the lean months. Shouldn't we hold Tarbela above 10% of its live capacity in every single month so we never run it that thin? What's the model giving up by drawing it down so far?
expected_pyomo: model.tarbela_floor_10pct = Constraint(model.m, rule=lambda mm, mo: mm.rcont['tarbela-r', mo] >= 0.10 * mm.rcap['tarbela-r'])

model_narrative: We operate the Indus surface-water network over a calendar year, month by month. The network is a system of nodes, river reaches, link canals, reservoirs, and irrigation-canal offtakes. For every month we decide the flow carried on each reach, the water diverted into each irrigation canal, and the end-of-month storage held in each reservoir, together with a single figure for the total stored volume across the system. The objective is to maximize the total live reservoir storage accumulated over all reservoirs and all months.

sets.name: n
sets.doc: all nodes of the river network: barrages, reservoirs, headworks, river-link junctions, and the artificial sea outlet a-sea; reservoir nodes carry live storage, the others are pass-through junctions

sets.name: nb
sets.doc: the nodes that must satisfy a water balance; this is every node except the sea outlet a-sea, which only receives terminal river outflow and is not balanced

sets.name: c
sets.doc: the irrigation canals that draw water off the network at their head nodes

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the system inflow points, the rim stations where the rivers enter the modeled network

sets.name: m
sets.doc: the twelve calendar months in chronological order; the month before january is december, so the reservoir storage cycle wraps around the year

sets.name: s
sets.doc: the two cropping seasons

sets.name: nc
sets.doc: the node-to-canal incidence pairs; a pair means that canal draws its water at that node, so that node loses the canal's diversion

sets.name: nn
sets.doc: the directed reach connectivity pairs; a pair from-node to-node means a river reach or link canal carries flow from the first node to the second

sets.name: ni
sets.doc: the node-to-inflow incidence pairs; a pair means that rim-station inflow enters the network at that node

params.name: ccap
params.doc: the capacity of each canal at its head, in million acre-feet per month

params.name: ncap
params.doc: the transfer capacity of the reach from the first node to the second, in million acre-feet per month; only defined on connected reaches

params.name: rcap
params.doc: the live storage capacity of each node, in million acre-feet; strictly positive only at the reservoir nodes and zero at every pass-through junction, so a positive value is what marks a node as a reservoir

params.name: ircont
params.doc: the initial reservoir contents used to anchor the storage cycle, in million acre-feet, defined at the reservoir nodes

params.name: inflow
params.doc: the rim-station inflow entering the network in each month, in million acre-feet

params.name: runoff
params.doc: the rainfall runoff added at a node in a month, in thousand acre-feet, so it must be divided by one thousand to convert to million acre-feet; defined only at the nodes and months that actually receive runoff, and absent elsewhere

params.name: revapl
params.doc: the evaporation loss from a reservoir in a month, in thousand acre-feet, so it must be divided by one thousand to convert to million acre-feet

params.name: rulelo
params.doc: the lower rule-curve level for a reservoir in a month, expressed as a percent of that reservoir's live capacity

params.name: ruleup
params.doc: the upper rule-curve level for a reservoir in a month, expressed as a percent of that reservoir's live capacity

params.name: trib
params.doc: the tributary inflow from the first node to the second in a month, in million acre-feet; sparse, so a node receives the sum over all source nodes and most entries are absent and count as zero

params.name: coeffl
params.doc: the conveyance efficiency of a link reach from the first node to the second in a month, a multiplier on the flow received at the downstream node; one for ordinary reaches

vars.name: rcont
vars.doc: the end-of-month live storage held at a node, in million acre-feet; meaningful only at reservoir nodes

vars.name: canaldiv
vars.doc: the water diverted into a canal in a month, in million acre-feet

vars.name: f
vars.doc: the flow on the reach from the first node to the second in a month, in million acre-feet

vars.name: vol
vars.doc: the total live reservoir storage summed over all reservoirs and all months, in million acre-feet

query: The whole network leans on a few big canals each month while the rest barely move water. Couldn't we floor it the other way — every active canal divert at least 0.5% of total monthly diversion so the load gets shared? What's stopping the model from spreading it around?
expected_pyomo: model.canal_min_share = Constraint(model.c, model.m, rule=lambda mm, c, mo: mm.canaldiv[c, mo] >= 0.005 * sum(mm.canaldiv[cc, mo] for cc in mm.c))
